name: Composure Drill — Handling the Unexpected
_note: Skill drill. Standard 4 metric keys re-pitched: medical_accuracy = appropriateness of the response, clinical_reasoning slot = composure & acknowledgement (the focus), communication_fluency = smooth recovery.

anchors.9-10: Clear, well-formed sentences even under pressure
anchors.7-8: Occasional minor errors; meaning always clear

anchors.9-10: Response fits the moment perfectly — safe, kind, and on-point
anchors.5-6: Mostly okay but a little off-key
anchors.3-4: Response often doesn't fit the situation

anchors.9-10: Stays calm, names/acknowledges the surprise, and handles it warmly
anchors.7-8: Composed; acknowledges the shift with minor lapses
anchors.5-6: A bit rattled but recovers; partial acknowledgement
anchors.3-4: Visibly thrown; ignores or glosses over the curveball

anchors.9-10: Smoothly absorbs the surprise and continues naturally
Exceptional empathy and professionalism; creates psychological safety
anchors.7-8: Good empathy; acknowledges emotion; polite and professional
anchors.5-6: Adequate but mechanical empathy; some emotional cues missed
anchors.3-4: Little empathic response; information delivery prioritized
anchors.1-2: No empathic response; dismissive or unprofessional

checklist.item: stayed calm and did not freeze when the curveball came
acknowledged the patient's sudden emotion or statement
responded with empathy before moving on
gently steered the conversation back on track
did NOT ignore or talk over the curveball
avoided panicking or making over-promises (e.g. 'everything will be fine')

empathy_markers: named or acknowledged the sudden emotion
